roasts.template: Let's circle back on your competence, but I'm afraid we'd need to invent the concept first.
Your LinkedIn says 'Thought Leader,' but your actual thoughts require a permission slip and three approvals.
You're not 'unemployed.' You're 'between opportunities' and 'between realities.'
You don't have a 5-year plan. You have a 5-minute excuse and a Pinterest board titled 'someday.'
Your resume has more buzzwords than achievements. Buzzwords: 47. Achievements: 👀.
You're the human equivalent of a corporate memo — long, unclear, and ignored by everyone with a job.
Your 'team player' status expired with your last performance review. HR didn't send a renewal.
You don't 'disrupt the industry.' You disrupt my patience and the office coffee machine.
Your elevator pitch has more ups and downs than your career. The elevator is filing a complaint.
You say 'synergy' the way normal people say 'please help me.' Both are equally hard to watch.
I've interviewed better candidates in my spam folder. At least they had the confidence to show up uninvited.
You treat meetings like attendance is the assignment. Spoiler: participation trophies don't promote.
You're a 'people person.' Unfortunately, no people have been confirmed. The people are also looking for you. To leave.
Your OKRs are a wish list your therapist is concerned about. The OKRs don't even know what they are. They are on a vision quest.
You say 'I wear many hats' but they all say 'Intern' on the tag. The hats are aspirational. The hats are also lying.
Your promotion is a software update that keeps rolling back. IT is concerned. HR is concerned. Your manager is updating their resume too.
You're 'passionate' the way a cover letter is 'passionate.' It's professional lying. The lie is consistent. The lie is on LinkedIn.
Your personal brand is 'unemployed with a website.' Squarespace has a special template for you. The template says 'in transition.' Forever.
You treat Slack status updates as a personality. 'Heads down' is not a personality. The status is also a lie. The status is also the job.
Your manager has a 1:1 with you every week. They are not happy. Their smile is in storage. Their agenda is your performance improvement plan.
You asked for feedback. The team gave it. You cried. Nobody was surprised. The tears were also feedback. HR saved them in a folder.
Your 'quick wins' take a quarter. The quarter is concerned. Q2 is preparing a complaint. The complaint is also taking a quarter.
You attend meetings about meetings about meetings. The matrix has you. Morpheus is also in the meeting. He's confused. He's also in another meeting.
Your performance review is a Mad Lib: 'Great energy, but [concern] about [skill].' [Skill] is everything. [Concern] is also everything. Both are about you.
